title: Leucanthemum kurilense
intro: Leucanthemum kurilense, also known as the Kuril daisy, is a perennial flowering plant native to the Kuril Islands in Russia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as its ability to support immune function and reduce oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Leucanthemum kurilense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Leucanthemum kurilense is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal medicine to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
taxonomy: Leucanthemum kurilense is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the genus Leucanthemum, which includes several species of daisies. The genus Leucanthemum is further classified within the tribe Anthemideae. Leucanthemum kurilense is native to the Kuril Islands and other parts of East Asia. This plant is often used in horticulture due to its attractive white flowers and compact growth habit.
